title: Tune ANN parameters against a recall target
description: Set HNSW or IVF build and search parameters from a measured recall-vs-latency curve, not from library defaults.
body: Use when building or tuning an approximate index. Do not accept default M, ef, or nprobe — defaults optimize for a generic case and may miss true neighbors on your data. Actions: build a labeled query set with ground-truth neighbors, sweep ef_search/nprobe (and build-time M/ef_construction), and plot recall against latency to pick a point that meets the recall floor. Validation signals include a recall-vs-latency benchmark and a chosen setting hitting the recall target inside the latency budget. Risk boundary: an under-tuned index silently drops relevant results; if recall cannot reach the floor within budget, escalate before serving rather than quietly accepting low recall.
